summary: The team is balancing several big tracks at once: tightening the digital marketing strategy and budget (AsiaStar, custom programs, remaining £5k, and a new 2026 plan), pushing toward a clearer content and VI story for the school, and opening up external partnerships that can amplify CKGSB’s brand. MediaMinds are helping refine LinkedIn/SEM/YouTube tactics and reporting, while the team keeps overdue video work and the May campus move on the radar. At the same time, they are exploring a strategic collaboration with Goldman Sachs, clarifying Blue Carrot and agency relationships, and being more disciplined about how many reports and campaigns they run.
summaryDetail:
Campaigns and budget: Over the past few meetings the team agreed a £43k split across AsiaStar, custom programs and brand awareness, shifted the social budget to a 90/10 LinkedIn/Instagram mix, and narrowed AsiaStar to three priority cities (Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, Kuala Lumpur). A fresh conversation with MediaMinds is shaping how to use the remaining £5k (likely via boosted posts and a possible journalist/editor segment) and how to test the custom program video without burning through the £8k.

Performance and reporting: MediaMinds are correcting a Supermetrics error in the LinkedIn spend report and preparing a more structured 2026 digital plan that covers LinkedIn, SEM and YouTube. Daisy and Joseph are tying campaign data back to leads and enrolments (Asia Start, Sydney 2025) so budget decisions are anchored in actual outcomes rather than just clicks.

Content, VI and product: The team is consolidating content under four themes (AI/technology, China going global, leadership, digital transformation) and capping themselves at 2–3 major reports a year, with a minimal-effort approach for the UN Global Compact report. VI work with six agencies and CSP/Blue Carrot video production are running in parallel, with clear sequencing (finish CSP video before launching display ads) and calendar moves (shifting Flo’s meetings around the March 4th pitches) to protect focus.

Partnerships and brand: New conversations with Goldman Sachs open the door to joint events (economic symposium, women in leadership, CEO programs), podcast appearances and curated roundtables in Hong Kong. At the same time, the team is renewing with MediaMinds while testing the market for agencies better aligned to Chinese companies going global, so they have options when the current contract cycle ends.

Operations and foundations: The new campus move in May 2026 remains a looming operational milestone. Across meetings, there is a consistent push to reduce scatter (fewer reports, clearer themes), clean up measurement, and keep decision-making anchored in both budget reality and long-term brand positioning.

meetings.title: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
Benefits points redemption walkthrough with colleague

CKGSB global positioning and strategic insights overview
Marketing content frequency and brand consistency review
South China Morning Post and CKGSB collaboration on Chinese companies going global index
SEO performance and campaign optimization review with Daisy
Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break

actionItems.text: Move Flo meetings to Friday for VI presentations
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Reschedule any meetings currently involving Flo so they fall on Friday instead of earlier in the week. Reason: Six agencies are presenting visual identity (VI) work on March 4th and the team needs those days clear to prepare and attend. Check calendars, propose new Friday slots, and send updated invites.

actionItems.text: Check with Knowledge team on CDP CEO interview value
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Reach out to the Knowledge team to confirm whether a CDP CEO interview fits their content priorities and what value they see in it (e.g. thought leadership, report tie-in). Get their view on format and timing before the team locks in the interview approach. This unblocks the parallel tasks on format and profile review.

actionItems.text: Review CDP CEO profile and brainstorm interview approach
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Review the CDP CEO's profile (background, expertise, recent work) and brainstorm 3–5 strong interview angles or themes that would work for our audience. Share with the team so the format decision (within 2 days) can be informed by concrete options. Coordinate with Joseph's check to Knowledge team on value.

actionItems.text: Coordinate on afternoon alignment meeting (after 5pm today)
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Schedule and hold a short alignment meeting between Joseph and Julie after 5pm (same day as the source meeting). Agenda: align on next steps for CDP interview, Blue Carrot follow-up, and VI presentation prep. Confirm owners and timelines so both can execute without overlap or gaps.

actionItems.text: Push Blue Carrot for video project response
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Proactively chase Blue Carrot (agency/vendor) for their response or deliverables on the video project(s). The projects are overdue; the goal is to get a clear reply (timeline, next steps, or blockers) so the team can unstick production. Anyone on the team can lead the follow-up; coordinate so it's one clear push.

actionItems.text: Decide on CDP interview format within 2 days
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: What it involves: As a team, decide the format for the CDP CEO interview within 2 days—e.g. written Q&A, short thought-leadership piece, or video. Consider Julie's proposed angles and Joseph's Knowledge team feedback. Once format is set, Joseph (value check) and Julie (profile/angles) can complete their tasks and production can be briefed.

actionItems.text: Compile Google, YouTube, and website performance data for 2025
actionItems.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy review for Europe and global markets
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Compile 2025 performance data from three channels: Google (e.g. Ads, Analytics), YouTube (e.g. views, engagement), and the website (e.g. traffic, conversions). Pull into a single summary (metrics, trends) so the team can use it for the Europe/global digital marketing strategy review and any reporting. Aim for clarity and consistency so it can sit alongside Julie's social data.

actionItems.text: Gather LinkedIn and Instagram results, include ad examples
actionItems.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy review for Europe and global markets
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Gather LinkedIn and Instagram performance results for 2025 (or the relevant period)—metrics, top posts/campaigns, and 2–3 concrete ad examples (creative plus short performance note). Format so it can sit alongside Joseph's Google, YouTube, and website data in the Europe/global digital marketing review. Include any spend or ROI figures if available.

actionItems.text: Evaluate purpose chapter via ChatGPT for translation viability
actionItems.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
actionItems.detail: What it involves: The team agreed to test whether the purpose chapter (from the report/content set) can be translated effectively using ChatGPT or similar tools. Your task is to run that evaluation—e.g. run a sample through the tool, check quality and consistency, and report back on viability. This will inform whether we use AI-assisted translation for the full chapter. Context: Discussed in project priorities and marketing campaign planning before the holiday break.

actionItems.text: Prioritize CSP video completion, then display ads launch
actionItems.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Stick to this order—(1) Complete the CSP video project first; (2) Then launch the display ads. Do not start display ads in parallel. Part of pre-holiday campaign planning so the team has a clear sequence and can track progress on each.

actionItems.text: Final video brief by Friday
actionItems.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Finalise the video brief (scope, key messages, format, timeline) and lock it by Friday so production can start. This brief feeds into the CSP video and other video work; getting it done unblocks the team and external partners.

actionItems.text: Asia Start LinkedIn campaign updates
actionItems.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Prepare and share updates on the Asia Start LinkedIn campaign—status, spend vs budget (£28k allocated), and performance in key markets (Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, Kuala Lumpur). Keep the team informed so decisions on timing and next phases can be made.

actionItems.text: Follow-up on MediaMinds contract and Instagram placement
actionItems.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Follow up with MediaMinds on the contract (terms, timeline, sign-off) and on the Instagram placement(s)—confirm specs, dates, and creative. This sits under the 90/10 LinkedIn/Instagram budget split; closing these items keeps the campaign on track.

actionItems.text: Schedule meeting for Feb 24-25 or week of March 2
actionItems.meetingTitle: Knowledge website design research and content strategy review
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Schedule a follow-up meeting for the knowledge website design and content strategy work. Target window: Feb 24–25 or the week of March 2. Invite the relevant people, set an agenda (e.g. next steps from the last session), and send the invite so the workstream keeps moving.

actionItems.text: Review both Chinese reports shared via chat
actionItems.meetingTitle: Meeting with Felix in two reports
actionItems.detail: What it involves: Review both Chinese reports that Felix shared in chat (read for content, quality, and fit with our strategy). Then provide clear feedback to Felix—what works, what to adjust, and how they tie into the two-reports plan and broader content strategy. Keeps the Felix collaboration and report pipeline aligned.

actionItems.text: Check with MediaMinds about budget figure discrepancy (reported vs actual spend)
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: LinkedIn backend shows one figure (e.g. 28.9) but MediaMinds report showed a different spend—affects how much we allocate to new campaigns. Need to align and get clarity from MediaMinds on how they calculate reported spend.

actionItems.text: Ask MediaMinds how to lower cost per click for custom program message ads
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Message ads for custom program are expensive per website visit (£250 per click mentioned). Get MediaMinds’ advice on improving cost effectiveness (e.g. extending period, narrowing audience, creative tweaks).

actionItems.text: Turn custom program contact into a form (region picker → right person)
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Add a form on the custom program page so visitors pick region (Americas/Europe/Asia); form submission goes to the right sales manager. Captures leads and improves tracking vs. current contact links.

actionItems.text: Develop boosting formula with MediaMinds (how many posts/month, budget per post)
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Make boosted posts a regular process: identify post types that perform (knowledge, faculty, etc.), agree with MediaMinds how many to boost per month and budget per post, so they proactively boost without weekly nudges.

actionItems.text: Joseph: ask Mara about Sydney program leads and sales conversion
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Follow up with Mara on leads and conversion from the Sydney/Regis Summit campaign so we can report ROI and inform future custom program spend.

actionItems.text: Proactively ask Mara, Bo, regional leads for campaign ROI and lead feedback
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Regularly follow up with regional sales (Mara, Bo, etc.) on leads from custom program and Asia Star campaigns so we can report results to leadership and optimize spend.

actionItems.text: Prepare short summary for Flo on the two POs (design and care) so he can approve
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Flo needs a brief synopsis of what the two POs are for (design and care) before he approves—so he can justify if asked. Provide that so he can sign off without delay.

actionItems.text: Ask MediaMinds why dynamic/follower acquisition ads haven’t started (assets provided)
actionItems.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
actionItems.detail: Follow up with MediaMinds: text/assets were provided for dynamic/follower acquisition ads; get timeline or blockers for launching them.

actionItems.text: Get a simple written plan from Flow on LLM optimisation for the website (schema, Q&A content, page priorities).
actionItems.meetingTitle: SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
actionItems.detail: In the Asia Start SEO/SEM review, Gontran outlined three LLM-related priorities (schema, Q&A content, and question-focused optimisation). Next step is for Flow to summarise this into a simple plan for CKGSB (what they will do and what content they need).

actionItems.text: Share CKGSB's 2026 digital marketing themes and plan with Flow so they can align SEM/SEO and LLM work.
actionItems.meetingTitle: SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
actionItems.detail: During the meeting, the team agreed that Flow needs CKGSB's 2026 themes (AI, China going global, economic outlook, etc.) and the overall digital marketing plan so they can propose the right LLM/SEO tactics.

actionItems.text: Prepare a short performance summary of Asia Start 2026 campaign for Associate Dean Fu and the Europe program team.
actionItems.meetingTitle: SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
actionItems.detail: By Friday, Daisy will compile an overview of the Asia Start 2026 campaign to date (spend, clicks, key conversions by region) so Joseph and Julie can present it at next week's meeting with Associate Dean Fu and the Europe team.

actionItems.text: Produce a Sydney 2025 campaign summary comparing performance by region and conversion type, and benchmark it vs Asia Start 2025.
actionItems.meetingTitle: SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
actionItems.detail: The team asked Daisy to summarise the Sydney 2025 SEM/SEO campaign: total clicks, leads by region and conversion type, and a comparison with last year's Asia Start campaign so they can judge relative performance.

actionItems.text: Ask Sydney program team how many participants came from SEM/SEO campaigns so results can be tied back to leads.
actionItems.meetingTitle: SEO and SEM campaign performance review for Asia start 2026
actionItems.detail: Joseph agreed to follow up with the Sydney program team to find out how many enrolled participants came from the SEM/SEO campaigns, so marketing can link campaign results to actual enrolments.

actionItems.text: Goldman to send list of speaker names and topics for economic symposium (June Beijing).
actionItems.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
actionItems.detail: Goldman will provide a list of content specialists who can speak (e.g. asset allocation, succession planning, trusts) and the topics they cover, so CKGSB can consider them for the June economic symposium. Best to have them do a short session for CKGSB first to review content.

actionItems.text: Follow up with past event info: economic symposium agenda/speakers, women in leadership, CEO program/EMBA details.
actionItems.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
actionItems.detail: CKGSB to send Goldman last year's economic symposium agenda and speakers, women in leadership forum info, and more detail on CEO program and EMBA student profile so Goldman can assess fit for collaboration.

actionItems.text: Goldman to send annual family office report to CKGSB.
actionItems.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
actionItems.detail: Goldman will share their annual family office report to inform topics and content for potential joint events (e.g. asset allocation, digital assets).

actionItems.text: Send professor backgrounds for Goldman podcast consideration (Exchanges etc.).
actionItems.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
actionItems.detail: Provide Goldman with professor backgrounds and topics they can speak on so Goldman can check with their podcast team (e.g. Exchanges) for potential external speaker slots; focus on insights/topics, not hard sell on the school.

actionItems.text: Create WeChat group and share initial info by email; Goldman to send email first then add to group.
actionItems.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
actionItems.detail: Day-to-day communication via WeChat; larger materials by email. Goldman to send initial email and create WeChat group with all participants.

actionItems.text: MediaMinds to send updated report with corrected LinkedIn spend figures.
actionItems.meetingTitle: New note
actionItems.detail: Supermetrics had duplicated a LinkedIn line in the report; headline spend numbers are being corrected. Analysis and findings remain valid. MediaMinds will send the updated report and ensure the issue does not recur.

actionItems.text: Sarah and Dave to propose how to allocate the remaining £5k (boosted posts, display, journalist audience test).
actionItems.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
actionItems.detail: MediaMinds asked Sarah and Dave to come back with thoughts on how to spend the remaining £5k. Options discussed: boosted posts (program to executives, knowledge to journalists), display ads, or testing journalist/editor audience targeting.

actionItems.text: MediaMinds to watch the custom program video and advise on testing it with part of the £8k.
actionItems.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
actionItems.detail: CKGSB want to test the custom program video with ad spend. MediaMinds will review the video and get back with a view on putting a small portion of the £8k into a video test.

actionItems.text: MediaMinds to share 2026 digital marketing plan and initial allocation ideas, then arrange a call with CKGSB.
actionItems.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
actionItems.detail: MediaMinds are preparing a detailed plan (LinkedIn, SEM, YouTube ads) for internal management. Once agreed, they will share initial ideas on ’26 budget allocation and program coverage and set a call to discuss with CKGSB.

decisions.text: Minimal effort approach for UN Global Compact report - use existing Chinese report, change cover
decisions.detail: Rather than producing new content for the UN Global Compact report, the team agreed to reuse the existing Chinese report and only change the cover. This reduces workload and aligns with the decision to focus on 2–3 reports maximum. Saves time for other priorities like the CDP interview and video work.

decisions.text: Keep knowledge and global websites separate (not merged)
decisions.meetingTitle: Knowledge website design research and content strategy review
decisions.detail: After reviewing the knowledge website and content strategy, the team decided not to merge the knowledge and global websites into one. Keeping them separate preserves distinct audiences and content flows, and avoids a large migration. Follow-up meetings were planned for Feb 24–25 or the week of March 2.

decisions.text: Budget allocation: £28k to AsiaStar campaign, £10k to custom programs, £5.3k for brand awareness
decisions.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
decisions.detail: The remaining £43k marketing budget is split three ways: £28k for the AsiaStar LinkedIn campaign (focus markets: Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, Kuala Lumpur), £10k for custom programs, and £5.3k for brand awareness. This sits within the broader 90/10 LinkedIn/Instagram campaign strategy.

decisions.text: Focus on 2-3 reports maximum (did 4 in prior year, too many)
decisions.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
decisions.detail: The team reflected that doing four reports in the prior year was too many and diluted quality. Going forward, cap at 2–3 reports so each gets proper attention. This also supports the minimal-effort approach for the UN Global Compact report (reuse existing Chinese report).

decisions.text: Campaign strategy: Shift to 90/10 budget split (LinkedIn/Instagram) from 80/20
decisions.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
decisions.detail: LinkedIn performance justified shifting more spend there: from an 80/20 split (LinkedIn/Instagram) to 90/10. Instagram remains in the mix but at a smaller share. Julie is following up on MediaMinds contract and Instagram placement within this new split.

decisions.text: Geographic focus for Asia Start: Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, Kuala Lumpur
decisions.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
decisions.detail: Asia Start campaign focus is narrowed to three high-potential markets: Jakarta, Ho Chi Minh City, and Kuala Lumpur. This concentrates the £28k AsiaStar budget and makes measurement and optimisation more manageable. Julie is responsible for Asia Start LinkedIn campaign updates.

decisions.text: Consolidate content strategy around four key themes: AI/technology, China going global, leadership, and digital transformation
decisions.detail: To give content a clear direction, the team agreed on four overarching themes: AI/technology, China going global, leadership, and digital transformation. New reports, articles, and the CDP CEO interview should align with these. Reduces fragmentation and helps position CKGSB consistently.

decisions.text: Complete CSP video before launching display ads (strict order of operations)
decisions.meetingTitle: Project priorities and marketing campaign planning before holiday break
decisions.detail: The team agreed a strict sequence: (1) finish the CSP video project, then (2) launch display ads. No parallel work on display ads until CSP is done. Joseph is prioritising the video brief by Friday so production can proceed. This avoids spreading capacity too thin before the holiday break.

decisions.text: CDP CEO interview format to be decided within two days to unblock Joseph and Julie's tasks
decisions.detail: A two-day deadline was set to decide the CDP CEO interview format (e.g. Q&A, thought-leadership piece, or video). Once format is fixed, Joseph can complete the value check with the Knowledge team and Julie can finalise the profile review and interview angles. The team will consider both inputs when deciding.

decisions.text: Move Flo's meetings to Friday to free up the week for VI agency presentations on March 4th
decisions.detail: Six agencies are presenting visual identity (VI) work on March 4th. To keep that week clear for prep and attendance, any meetings involving Flo are to be moved to Friday. Joseph is responsible for rescheduling and sending updated invites.

decisions.text: Include boosted posts in ongoing LinkedIn strategy; get budget number from MediaMinds
decisions.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
decisions.detail: Team agreed to make boosted posts a regular part of the campaign (not just on request). Julie to work with MediaMinds on how many posts per month and how much budget per boosted post; use best-performing organic content (knowledge, faculty posts) as the basis.

decisions.text: Renew with MediaMinds but simultaneously look for other agencies (procurement)
decisions.meetingTitle: LinkedIn marketing campaign budget allocation and performance review
decisions.detail: Continue contract renewal with MediaMinds (they are not terrible and 20% is standard) but in parallel explore other agencies, especially those focused on Chinese companies going global, as procurement has suggested. If a better option emerges, can switch in ’27.

decisions.text: Explore CKGSB–Goldman collaboration via economic symposium (June), speakers, Hong Kong roundtable, and podcast.
decisions.meetingTitle: Potential Goldman Sachs and CKGSB collaboration strategies
decisions.detail: Both sides agreed to explore collaboration: Goldman content specialists speaking at CKGSB events (e.g. June economic symposium), possible co-created Hong Kong roundtable, and CKGSB faculty on Goldman podcasts. Next steps are speaker lists, past event info, and WeChat/email follow-up.

decisions.text: Do not extend Asia Start campaign on same budget; consider additional funds if extending.
decisions.detail: MediaMinds advised against simply extending the Asia Start campaign with the existing pot—daily budget would drop and hurt ad delivery. They could support an extension with additional funds from this financial year if CKGSB wants to run longer.

decisions.text: Use boosted posts for remaining funds; Sarah and Dave to propose allocation of remaining £5k
decisions.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
decisions.detail: For the limited remaining budget, boosted posts are a good option (e.g. program post to executives, knowledge post to journalists). Display ads may consume funds quickly. Sarah and Dave to come back with thoughts on how to allocate the remaining £5k.

decisions.text: Test custom program video with a small part of £8k; English first, then consider localization
decisions.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
decisions.detail: Message ads remain primary for the HR/corporate audience; video is secondary. Team agreed to test the custom program video with a portion of the £8k. MediaMinds to review and advise. Test English version first; consider localised subtitles (e.g. Indonesian/English) later based on who engages and which companies they are in.

decisions.text: Consider journalist/editor audience as a targeting strand to build awareness
decisions.meetingTitle: Digital marketing strategy for remaining budget and video ad testing
decisions.detail: Cost up and test targeting journalists and editors for a few weeks (e.g. boosted knowledge posts) so they are aware of CKGSB as a source for China business, AI, education content. Part of the thinking for allocating the remaining £5k.
